memories.content: User considers Plan C the safest option for their school project backup, as it involves carrying nothing and explaining nothing, reducing the chance of getting flagged.
User emphasizes that people often exacerbate a minor issue, like a bad hose, into a major engine problem by being stubborn.
User prefers a spending plan that specifies exact amounts rather than percentages, suggesting a split of 20% to spend now, 70% to be locked away, and 10% as a buffer.
User wants to set aside around $30 for car repairs and has decided not to pay for any repair that costs more than $50 themselves.

User wants a detailed list of exact bead combinations for their custom phone charms and keychains, specifying the exact colors and shapes for each design, including which design should be priced at $3 as the easiest seller and which one to produce the most of if the first batch sells.
User noted that the flashing check engine light and issues with brakes/steering are the main problems people often overlook when troubleshooting car issues.
User has a little money set aside from birthdays and is trying to figure out the smartest way to split it between spending a bit now and keeping some for later if something comes up.

User wants a small price chart for their custom phone charms and keychains, including materials cost, time to make, and suggested selling prices to ensure they move quickly at school without underpricing.

User is seeking a more concrete plan to engage their robotics club group, focusing on specific actions to take when the group is stalling on coding, including what to say first, how to quickly split coding tasks, and how to maintain control during discussions.
User needs to send a quick group chat message about a change in their school plan and wants it to be clear enough for everyone to understand what’s happening.

User needs three specific backup setups for their school project that are low-key and avoid a business vibe, including details on where to keep the supplies, when to conduct the activities, and what to say if questioned.

User needs a specific order for tackling their school assignment and smaller tasks, asking for a sequence that prioritizes the fastest 5-10 minute task first, followed by the easiest part of the assignment, then the next small task if it's still short, and a hard stop rule to switch tasks after a set time or if they find themselves rereading the same section.
User decided to cut beads from their budget first and will only purchase snacks if there is still room after covering school-related expenses and the phone charger cable.
User emphasizes the importance of keeping responses tight and factual in a school situation, advising against volunteering extra information, making up stories, or naming people unless necessary.

User wants a decision tree for their backup plan for leaving school later than usual on April 29, 2026, detailing actions based on timing: if 10 minutes late, text the first person once; if no reply in 5 minutes, text the second person; if 20 minutes late, go to the front office and stay there; if 30 minutes late or no confirmed pickup, use a backup ride or ask for a late bus, and if neither is available, stay with staff until someone arrives.
User needs to write a short introduction for a school club meeting where everyone shares a little about themselves, and they want it to be memorable in an informal setting.

User's decision tree for their backup plan for leaving school later than usual on April 29, 2026, specifies actions based on timing: if 10 minutes late, text the first person once; if 15 minutes late, text the second person once; if 20 minutes late, go to the front office and stay there; if 30 minutes late, use a backup ride or ask for a late bus; if no confirmed pickup, stay with staff and avoid wandering.

User needs a specific order for completing their school assignment and smaller tasks on April 29, 2026, emphasizing the need for the actual order based on their specific tasks. They want to complete a quick annoying task first if it truly takes 5 minutes, followed by the exact first move on the assignment, then the next small task only if it remains short, and to stop when they have been stuck rereading for 2 minutes or if a small task takes 10 minutes.

User prefers a concise plan that outlines specific actions to take based on the timing of their ride, such as what to do if their ride is 10 minutes late, 20 minutes late, or falls through completely.

User wants a clear handoff setup for their robotics project, detailing where the code should be stored, who is responsible for checking it, and what needs to be completed before the final submission to avoid last-minute issues.

User wants specific wording for handling conversations with different types of adults in a school situation: for a teacher, a short answer with no drama; for a counselor, a little more detail but not a full confession; for a parent, to be careful as they might escalate the situation; and for an administrator, to be factual and avoid attitude.

User's trip plan from Los Lunas to Albuquerque this weekend includes stopping at Walmart on Coors Blvd NW first, then visiting a doctor's office near Presbyterian downtown at 1st and Coal, and returning on I-25 S to Los Lunas.

User's planned route includes watching for I-25 N out of Los Lunas, I-40 / Coors area for the west-side stop, Downtown / Central / 1st St / Coal for the doctor's office, and the I-25 / I-40 interchange if switching sides of town.
User needs a practical backup plan for their school project that complies with school rules, preferring options that are low-key and easy to hide or conduct on their own time.
User needs to plan a trip from Los Lunas to several locations in Albuquerque over the weekend and is seeking help to determine the simplest route and key roads or landmarks to watch for.
User has a total of $86 for their two-week budget.

User wants the format for their custom phone charms and keychains to include: charm 1 with exact beads and count plus price, charm 2 with exact beads and count plus price, charm 3 with exact beads and count plus price, along with a tiny buy list for the first batch to avoid leftovers.
User prefers short and clear messages for group chats about school projects, finding them usable and not weird.
User prefers not to zigzag across town twice during their trip to Albuquerque this weekend.
User mentioned that if one of the places is closer to Central / I-40, it would complicate the route compared to if it’s near Rio Bravo or Uptown.

User's criteria for deciding which handmade accessories to continue making include keeping items that sell fast and are easy to remake, making fewer of items that sell but are difficult to produce, and dropping items that do not sell unless there is consistent demand.

User emphasizes the importance of deciding whether to admit knowledge of a situation or to maintain a stance of 'I don’t know' if unsure, indicating that this aspect is more critical than the categories themselves.
User needs to create a backup plan for leaving school later than usual on April 29, 2026, to avoid being stuck waiting if their ride falls through.

User's decision-making framework for handling school situations now includes a seriousness split: if the situation is low-key, they usually watch; if it's tense, they start collecting exact details; and if it's already ugly, they act immediately.

User is seeking a fast troubleshooting checklist for their ride if it starts acting weird on the way home, including specific actions for overheating/steam, brakes/steering, weird noise/shaking, losing power, and check engine light.

User found the yes/no decision point logic for identifying weak points in their small project useful, specifically the breakdown: 'Breaks on its own = materials / works only when forced = fit / fits but still fails = setup'.

User wants a few more versions of their scholarship/application response that are plainspoken and not polished, emphasizing reasons like saving money, gaining a useful skill, or doing something worthwhile, while keeping the responses as short as possible.
User suggested a sharper version of their introduction for the school club meeting: 'I’m [Name]. I joined because I’d rather get things done than just sit around talking.'
User is thinking about selling a few handmade accessories and is seeking help to develop a simple method for setting prices and deciding when to adjust them based on sales performance.
User confirmed that the order for tackling their school assignment and smaller tasks is the sequence they were looking for, emphasizing the need for a specific order based on their tasks.
User wants a one-line template for handling their part in a group project, along with a line to use when others start acting like it's their fault.
User wants a tighter backup plan for rides and money in case their family's car needs repair, focusing on realistic amounts for a teenager and specific actions to take if the car breaks down again.
User wants a detailed build order for making a charm or keychain from leftover materials, specifying the order of steps and the point at which they should stop and fix issues instead of continuing.
User wants to refine their decision-making framework for handling school situations by including how serious the situation looks right now, categorizing it as low-key, tense, or already ugly.

User wants a tighter spending plan that specifies exact numbers based on their available funds, aiming to avoid leaving a lot of money sitting around where it could be used up. They prefer to have a portion locked away for emergencies rather than just saved in a vague sense.

User's robotics club has a build coming up, and the group is stalling on the coding part as the deadline approaches. User is seeking help to find a way to step in, keep the project moving, and manage the handoff to ensure the final submission is organized.
User has mixed craft supplies and is looking for a concrete method for laying them out instead of a list of categories.
User needs to post a quick message in a group chat about a school project and wants it to sound clear and concise.

User has a school event coming up where they need to decide whether to jump in quickly or wait and observe, and they are seeking help to think through the situation to determine the best course of action.

User confirmed that they can use the provided plan for their project as-is.

User is considering the type of adult involved in a school situation, such as a teacher, counselor, parent, or administrator, and whether the issue relates to something they did, something they saw, or someone else.

User prefers a simple scoring method for comparing project ideas in class, using a tiny table with three criteria: time, cost, and feasibility of completion. They suggest using a green/yellow/red system for quick assessment and recommend the risk factor only if two ideas are tied.
User is trying to figure out the safest way to handle a school situation involving an adult, seeking guidance on what to do first and what to avoid to prevent making the situation worse.
User has a school project where the group is behind and is seeking a simple method to sort out the next steps and keep the work organized.

User wants a detailed shopping list for their custom phone charms and keychains, including the exact bead counts for each color needed for the first batch, which design should be stocked heavier if it sells, and a format specifying the exact beads, counts, and prices for each charm.
User's class needs to choose one project idea from several options within a short time frame and is seeking a simple method to compare the ideas for a quick decision.

User finds the 'most blocking task first' approach useful for sorting out next steps in their school project, emphasizing that knowing what unlocks the rest is crucial to avoid stagnation.
User finds the 'skip it and come back' approach useful when they encounter dragging tasks, as it prevents wasting time.

User is seeking a specific sequence for tackling their school assignment and smaller tasks, emphasizing the need for the actual order based on their specific tasks rather than a generic template. They want to prioritize a 5-minute annoying small task before the hardest assignment if it won't become a time sink, and they need the exact first move for the assignment instead of just the easiest piece.

User is seeking a tighter sequence for tackling their school assignment and smaller tasks, asking for a specific order of actions to achieve the fastest win first. They want to know what to complete in 10 minutes, what to ignore until later, and when to stop refining a task and move on.

User's backup plan for their school project includes three fallback options: Plan A involves bringing one sample after school in a pencil pouch or envelope only if someone has already asked about it. Plan B is a notes-only approach, keeping it to initials and items without a full list, to be checked at home later. Plan C is a home-only follow-up, where no notebook is taken to school, and the user tells others they will answer later and handle it off campus.
User believes that the time required to make an item is crucial for pricing handmade accessories, stating that if something takes a long time, it needs to be priced accordingly to be worth making.

User is seeking a decision point checklist for identifying weak points in their small project, requesting a yes/no flow to determine if issues are related to materials, fit, or setup, along with suggestions for the first change to make in each case.

User's pricing strategy for handmade accessories includes three categories: keep making more for items that sell fast, are easy to remake, and have a good margin; raise the price or make fewer of items that sell but take time; and cut items that do not sell and waste materials.

User prefers a method for tackling their school assignment that involves making small moves first without trying to perfect anything before starting. They plan to use a 2-minute reset strategy if they get stuck, which includes opening the assignment, copying the prompt into notes, listing what needs to be turned in, writing an easy first sentence or bullet, and continuing in short chunks.

User wants to add 'annoyance level' to their pricing table for handmade accessories, indicating that even if an item takes a reasonable amount of time, if it is fiddly to make, it should be factored into the pricing strategy.
